questions.question: Explain Partial and Required and its primary use case in TypeScript.
questions.answer: Partial and Required provides a structured mechanism to handle core application logic cleanly.

questions.question: What are common mistakes developers make with Partial and Required?
questions.answer: Common issues include scoping errors, unhandled boundary cases, and improper resource cleanup.

questions.question: How does the TypeScript runtime optimize Partial and Required under the hood?
questions.answer: The runtime engine uses symbol tables, call stack frames, and bytecode optimization to run instructions efficiently.
